title: alysicarpus heterophyllus
intro: Alysicarpus heterophyllus, also known as the hairy dog's tail, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Alysicarpus heterophyllus include its ability to treat fever, sore throat, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are typically used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions that are administered orally to harness its medicinal properties.
